 {"id":"bd-1ho.7","title":"Implement logging integration","description":"# Implement logging integration (rich-style formatting on top of tracing)

## Objective
Provide an optional logging layer that formats `tracing` events into styled output (Segments) in a way that is:
- readable for humans
- deterministic for tests
- compatible with ftui’s one-writer routing

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/logging.rs`

## Scope
- A formatter/subscriber (or layer) that:
  - applies per-level styling (error/warn/info/debug/trace)
  - supports timestamps (configurable)
  - optionally shows source location (file:line)
  - formats structured fields consistently

## One-Writer Rule (Critical)
- This integration must not write directly to stdout/stderr in a way that can interleave with ftui rendering.
- Output should be routed into a sink that can be:
  - captured for tests, or
  - forwarded via ftui’s `LogSink`/TerminalWriter policy.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for formatting choices (stable snapshots of formatted lines/segments).
- Concurrency test: multiple threads logging cannot interleave within a single formatted record.
- Integration test (optional) that shows logs routed into the harness log region.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A tracing integration exists (subscriber/layer) with configurable formatting.
- [ ] Output is Segment-based and can be captured deterministically in tests.
- [ ] One-writer routing is respected (no hidden direct terminal writes).
- [ ] Unit tests cover formatting and level styling.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:29.952985639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:15:48.513357374Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:15:48.513267144Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","depends_on_id":"bd-10x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3e8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":314,"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ho.8","title":"Rich Core Infrastructure Test Suite","description":"Comprehensive test suite for rich_rust core infrastructure. These foundational components support all renderables and require thorough verification.

## Test Requirements by Module

### Segment System (bd-1ho.1)
Unit tests:
- `test_segment_new_owned` - Owned string segment
- `test_segment_new_borrowed` - Borrowed string segment (Cow)
- `test_segment_control` - Control segment creation
- `test_segment_cell_length_ascii` - ASCII width = byte length
- `test_segment_cell_length_unicode` - Wide chars counted correctly
- `test_segment_cell_length_control` - Control segments = 0 width
- `test_segment_split_at_ascii` - Split ASCII segment
- `test_segment_split_at_unicode` - Split at grapheme boundary
- `test_segment_strip_styles` - Remove styles preserve text
- `test_segments_join` - Concatenate segments
- `test_segments_split_lines` - Split on newlines
- `test_segments_join_lines` - Rejoin lines

Property tests:
- `prop_split_join_inverse` - Split then join = original
- `prop_cell_length_non_negative` - Cell length >= 0
- `prop_split_preserves_total_length` - Sum of parts = whole

### Console (bd-1ho.2)
Unit tests:
- `test_console_default_width` - Width detection or fallback
- `test_console_explicit_width` - Override width
- `test_console_print_string` - Simple string output
- `test_console_print_styled` - Styled text output
- `test_console_print_renderable` - Renderable output
- `test_console_style_stack_push` - Push style
- `test_console_style_stack_pop` - Pop style
- `test_console_style_stack_nested` - Nested styles cascade
- `test_console_color_system_detect` - Auto-detect color capability
- `test_console_no_color_env` - NO_COLOR respected
- `test_console_force_color` - Force color override
- `test_console_capture_begin` - Start recording
- `test_console_capture_end` - Retrieve captured segments

Integration tests:
- `test_console_with_renderables` - Full rendering pipeline
- `test_console_line_wrapping` - Long lines wrapped

### Measurement (bd-1ho.3)
Unit tests:
- `test_measurement_new_valid` - min <= max
- `test_measurement_from_width` - min = max = width
- `test_measurement_span` - max - min
- `test_measurement_with_maximum` - Clamp maximum
- `test_measurement_with_minimum` - Clamp minimum
- `test_measure_text_single_line` - Single line width
- `test_measure_text_multiline` - Max of all lines
- `test_measure_text_with_wrap` - Account for wrap points

Property tests:
- `prop_measurement_min_le_max` - min always <= max
- `prop_measurement_clamp_valid` - Clamping preserves invariant

### Box Drawing (bd-1ho.4)
Unit tests:
- `test_box_rounded_chars` - Rounded box characters
- `test_box_square_chars` - Square box characters  
- `test_box_double_chars` - Double-line characters
- `test_box_heavy_chars` - Heavy box characters
- `test_box_ascii_chars` - ASCII fallback
- `test_box_substitute_true` - Returns ASCII variant
- `test_box_substitute_false` - Returns original
- `test_box_get_top` - Top border string
- `test_box_get_bottom` - Bottom border string
- `test_box_get_row_single` - Single cell row
- `test_box_get_row_multiple` - Multi-cell row with separators

### Live Display (bd-1ho.5)
Unit tests:
- `test_live_start` - Begin live display
- `test_live_stop` - End live display
- `test_live_update` - Replace content
- `test_live_refresh` - Redraw current content
- `test_live_vertical_overflow_ellipsis` - Truncate with ...
- `test_live_vertical_overflow_crop` - Hard crop
- `test_live_transient` - Cursor save/restore

Integration tests:
- `test_live_with_progress` - Progress bar in live context
- `test_live_nested` - Nested live displays

### Filesize Formatter (bd-1ho.6)
Unit tests:
- `test_filesize_bytes` - < 1KB shows bytes
- `test_filesize_kb_binary` - KiB formatting
- `test_filesize_mb_binary` - MiB formatting
- `test_filesize_gb_binary` - GiB formatting
- `test_filesize_tb_binary` - TiB formatting
- `test_filesize_decimal` - 1000-based units
- `test_filesize_precision` - Decimal places
- `test_filesize_short` - Short unit names
- `test_filesize_edge_zero` - Zero bytes
- `test_filesize_edge_max` - u64::MAX

Property tests:
- `prop_filesize_parses_back` - Formatted string can be parsed

### Logging Integration (bd-1ho.7)
Unit tests:
- `test_log_level_styling` - Error=red, warn=yellow, info=blue
- `test_log_timestamp_format` - Timestamp in expected format
- `test_log_source_location` - file:line shown
- `test_log_structured_fields` - Key=value pairs
- `test_log_quiet_mode` - Reduced output
- `test_log_with_live` - Logs don't corrupt live display

Integration tests:
- `test_log_tracing_subscriber` - Works with tracing

## E2E Test Scenarios

### Console Pipeline E2E
```rust
#[test]
fn e2e_console_full_render() {
    // Setup
    let mut console = Console::with_width(80);
    console.begin_capture();
    
    // Render complex content
    console.rule(Some("Header"));
    console.print(Panel::new(Text::from("Content")).with_title("Box"));
    console.print(Columns::new(vec![col1, col2]));
    console.rule(None);
    
    // Verify
    let segments = console.end_capture();
    assert!(!segments.is_empty());
    assert!(segments.iter().any(|s| s.text.contains("Header")));
    // ... more assertions
}
```

### Measurement Chain E2E
```rust
#[test]
fn e2e_measurement_chain() {
    let content = Group::new(vec![
        Box::new(Padding::new(
            Panel::new(Text::from("Inner")),
            Sides::all(2)
        )),
        Box::new(Rule::new()),
    ]);
    
    let measurement = content.measure(&console, 80);
    assert!(measurement.minimum <= measurement.maximum);
    assert!(measurement.maximum <= 80);
    
    let rendered = content.render(&console, 80);
    for line in &rendered.lines {
        let width: usize = line.iter().map(|s| s.cell_length()).sum();
        assert!(width <= 80);
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- `tracing::info!` for console output operations
- `tracing::debug!` for measurement calculations
- `tracing::trace!` for segment construction
- Live display refreshes logged at trace level

## Coverage Targets
- Line coverage: >= 90%
- Branch coverage: >= 85%

Dependencies: All Rich Core modules, Testing infrastructure (bd-10i.11)


## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Covers unit/snapshot/property tests for each rich-derived extra we keep.
- [ ] Includes at least one integration test demonstrating one-writer safe routing.
- [ ] Failure output is actionable (expected vs actual segments/lines).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:17:50.143443525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:55:47.496218399Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:55:47.496051785Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":315,"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening;  {"id":"bd-1hod6","title":"Fix Quake renderer fog/light blending quantization regression","description":"Audit found ftui-extras quake renderer optimization fused lighting+fog channels and moved u8 quantization after fog mix. This changes pixel output versus legacy path (e.g. low-intensity/fog boundary cases), violating no-visual-change optimization intent and risking checksum drift. Restore legacy quantization semantics while keeping safe perf improvements, and add regression test for quantization-before-fog behavior.","notes":"Started after deep audit of concurrent-agent changes; root cause isolated to post-fog quantization shift in quake renderer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"WildMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:55:25.359363670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:03:13.532175835Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T01:03:13.532156018Z","close_reason":"Restored quake renderer quantization-before-fog semantics to avoid visual drift; added regression test; validated with cargo test -p ftui-extras --features quake quake::renderer and cargo fmt --check. -{"id":"bd-1i0ib","title":"bug(ftui-core): enforce single active TerminalSession","description":"TerminalSession contract says exclusive ownership (only one session at a time) but code does not enforce it.

Implement a process-wide guard so TerminalSession::new() returns an error if another live TerminalSession exists. Avoid breaking parallel/unit tests by not enforcing this for new_for_tests (test-helpers).

Add focused tests to prove:
- second TerminalSession::new_for_tests(...) is allowed (headless)
- TerminalSession::new() rejects concurrent creation (cfg-gated when terminal backend available)
- guard clears on Drop so creating, dropping, then creating again works.

Rationale: correctness and preventing undefined terminal behavior.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"WindyLynx","created_at":"2026-02-10T19:08:06.124768988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:08:20.052015781Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1i67","title":"Animation Choreographer","description":"## Overview
Provide high-level animation composition system for coordinating complex multi-element animations.

## Rationale
- Individual animations are simple but coordination is hard
- Staggered, parallel, and sequential timing needed
- Animation groups should share lifecycle
- Enables polished onboarding and transitions

## Core Components
1. **Timeline**: Sequence of animation events with timing
2. **Animation Group**: Multiple animations with shared control
3. **Stagger**: Apply delay between group members
4. **Callbacks**: Events at animation milestones
5. **Presets**: Common patterns (fade-in-group, cascade, etc.)

## Implementation Strategy
- Build on existing Animation types in ftui-widgets
- Create Choreographer in ftui-extras
- Integrate with Tick events from ftui-runtime
- Provide builder API for timeline construction

## Usage Example
Choreographer::new()
    .add(fade_in(header), Duration::ZERO)
    .add_staggered(items.map(slide_in), ms(50))
    .on_complete(|| show_content())
    .start()

## Preset Animations
- cascade_in: Staggered entrance from top
- fan_out: Elements spread from center
- typewriter: Character-by-character reveal
- pulse_sequence: Attention-grabbing highlight

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for timeline calculation
- Snapshot tests at key animation frames
- Integration tests for callback timing
- Performance tests for large animation groups

## Success Criteria
- Complex animations can be composed declaratively
- Animations stay synchronized across elements
- Preset library covers common use cases

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-cyww","title":"Define DegradationLevel enum with 6 progressive levels","description":"# Define DegradationLevel enum with 6 progressive levels

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). Progressive degradation levels allow the system to maintain responsiveness by reducing visual fidelity under load.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Progressive degradation levels for render budget enforcement
/// Levels are ordered from highest fidelity to lowest
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Debug, Default)]
#[repr(u8)]
pub enum DegradationLevel {
    /// Full fidelity - all visual features enabled
    /// Unicode box-drawing, full styling, animations
    #[default]
    Full = 0,
    
    /// Skip expensive borders (Unicode box-drawing → ASCII +-)
    /// Still has colors and styling, just simpler borders
    SimpleBorders = 1,
    
    /// Skip styling (colors → monochrome)
    /// Text is readable but no color differentiation
    NoStyling = 2,
    
    /// Skip non-essential widgets (decorative only)
    /// Only functional content rendered
    EssentialOnly = 3,
    
    /// Skeleton render (just layout boxes)
    /// Shows structure but no content
    Skeleton = 4,
    
    /// Emergency: skip frame entirely
    /// Used when system is severely overloaded
    SkipFrame = 5,
}
```

## Implementation Details

```rust
impl DegradationLevel {
    /// Move to next degradation level (lower fidelity)
    pub fn degrade(&self) -> Self {
        match self {
            Self::Full => Self::SimpleBorders,
            Self::SimpleBorders => Self::NoStyling,
            Self::NoStyling => Self::EssentialOnly,
            Self::EssentialOnly => Self::Skeleton,
            Self::Skeleton | Self::SkipFrame => Self::SkipFrame,
        }
    }
    
    /// Move to previous degradation level (higher fidelity)
    pub fn upgrade(&self) -> Self {
        match self {
            Self::SkipFrame => Self::Skeleton,
            Self::Skeleton => Self::EssentialOnly,
            Self::EssentialOnly => Self::NoStyling,
            Self::NoStyling => Self::SimpleBorders,
            Self::SimpleBorders | Self::Full => Self::Full,
        }
    }
    
    /// Check if borders should use ASCII instead of Unicode
    pub fn use_ascii_borders(&self) -> bool {
        *self >= Self::SimpleBorders
    }
    
    /// Check if styling (colors) should be applied
    pub fn apply_styling(&self) -> bool {
        *self < Self::NoStyling
    }
    
    /// Check if non-essential widgets should render
    pub fn render_decorative(&self) -> bool {
        *self < Self::EssentialOnly
    }
    
    /// Check if content should render (vs skeleton)
    pub fn render_content(&self) -> bool {
        *self < Self::Skeleton
    }
    
    /// Check if frame should be skipped entirely
    pub fn skip_frame(&self) -> bool {
        *self >= Self::SkipFrame
    }
}
```

## Why This Design

1. **Ordered enum**: Levels are comparable (Full < SimpleBorders < ...)
2. **Bounded transitions**: degrade() and upgrade() never go out of bounds
3. **Query methods**: Widgets can easily check what features to enable
4. **Default = Full**: System starts at highest fidelity

## Test Requirements

```rust
#[test]
fn degradation_level_ordering() {
    assert!(DegradationLevel::Full < DegradationLevel::SimpleBorders);
    assert!(DegradationLevel::SimpleBorders < DegradationLevel::NoStyling);
    // ... all pairs
}

#[test]
fn degrade_never_goes_beyond_skip_frame() {
    let mut level = DegradationLevel::SkipFrame;
    for _ in 0..10 {
        level = level.degrade();
        assert_eq!(level, DegradationLevel::SkipFrame);
    }
}

#[test]
fn upgrade_never_goes_beyond_full() {
    let mut level = DegradationLevel::Full;
    for _ in 0..10 {
        level = level.upgrade();
        assert_eq!(level, DegradationLevel::Full);
    }
}

#[test]
fn query_methods_match_level() {
    assert!(!DegradationLevel::Full.use_ascii_borders());
    assert!(DegradationLevel::SimpleBorders.use_ascii_borders());
    // ... etc
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DegradationLevel enum defined with 6 variants
- [ ] Ord trait derived for comparison
- [ ] degrade()/upgrade() methods are bounded
- [ ] Query methods for all feature checks
- [ ] All tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:15:41.353025658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:14:28.690300649Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:14:28.690276413Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-1iqn in budget.rs - DegradationLevel with 6 levels (Full, SimpleBorders, NoStyling, EssentialOnly, Skeleton, SkipFrame)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cyww","depends_on_id":"bd-1iqn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-czn4y","title":"E2E: Text effects determinism sweep","description":"Scope: E2E sweep over text effects (gradient, wave, glitch, typewriter, matrix/ocean presets) to verify deterministic rendering.  {"id":"bd-dez5","title":"Implement Render Budget unit and property tests","description":"# Implement Render Budget unit and property tests

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). Comprehensive tests ensure budget enforcement works correctly.

## Unit Tests
- remaining_budget_decreases_over_time: Verify remaining() returns less as time passes
- degradation_level_ordering_is_correct: Verify Full < SimpleBorders < NoStyling < EssentialOnly < Skeleton < SkipFrame
- degrade_advances_to_next_level: Verify degrade() moves to next level
- degrade_stops_at_skip_frame: Verify degrade() at SkipFrame stays at SkipFrame
- upgrade_returns_to_previous_level: Verify upgrade() moves to previous level
- should_degrade_returns_true_when_cost_exceeds_remaining: Test budget decision logic
- exhausted_returns_true_when_skip_frame_or_zero_budget: Test exhausted() edge cases

## Property Tests
- degrade_upgrade_roundtrip: Degrading then upgrading returns to same or lower level
- budget_remaining_never_exceeds_total: remaining() is always <= total
- degradation_is_monotonic: Multiple degrade() calls never decrease level

## Integration Tests
- widget_respects_degradation_level: Panel/Text widgets adjust output based on level
- system_recovers_after_budget_exhaustion: After overload, system eventually upgrades back

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Property tests find no counterexamples
- [ ] Integration tests verify widget behavior
- [ ] Test coverage >= 90% for RenderBudget module","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:32:00.093642605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:17:55.980270238Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:17:55.980251403Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-1iqn in budget.rs - 34 tests covering degradation ordering, upgrade/downgrade roundtrip, remaining never exceeds total, exhausted edge cases","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dez5","depends_on_id":"bd-1iqn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-e211","title":"Implement Text wrapping and truncation with ZWJ/emoji/combining mark correctness","description":"# Implement Text Wrapping and Truncation with Width Correctness

## Background
Plan Section F and Chapter 11 emphasize that text wrapping and truncation must be WIDTH-CORRECT for ZWJ sequences, emoji, and combining marks. This is a non-negotiable for Unicode correctness.

## Problem Statement
Breaking text at arbitrary byte or codepoint boundaries corrupts display:
- ZWJ sequences (👨‍👩‍👧) are single graphemes but multiple codepoints
- Combining marks (é = e + ́) must stay with base char
- Regional indicators (🇺🇸) are pairs of codepoints
- CJK characters are 2 cells wide

## Requirements

### Wrapping Rules
1. Never break inside a grapheme cluster
2. Respect cell width (CJK = 2 cells)
3. Handle ZWJ sequences as atomic units
4. Word-wrap at whitespace when possible
5. Character-wrap as fallback for long words

### Truncation Rules
1. Truncate at grapheme boundary only
2. Ellipsis (…) counts as 1 cell
3. If content + ellipsis > width, drop graphemes until it fits
4. Never leave partial grapheme displayed

## Implementation

### Text Wrapping
```rust
pub fn wrap_text(text: &str, width: usize) -> Vec<String> {
    let mut lines = Vec::new();
    let mut current_line = String::new();
    let mut current_width = 0;
    
    for grapheme in text.graphemes(true) {
        let grapheme_width = unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(grapheme);
        
        if current_width + grapheme_width > width {
            // Line full, start new line
            if !current_line.is_empty() {
                lines.push(std::mem::take(&mut current_line));
            }
            current_width = 0;
        }
        
        current_line.push_str(grapheme);
        current_width += grapheme_width;
    }
    
    if !current_line.is_empty() {
        lines.push(current_line);
    }
    
    lines
}
```

### Text Truncation
```rust
pub fn truncate_text(text: &str, max_width: usize, ellipsis: &str) -> String {
    let text_width = unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(text);
    
    if text_width <= max_width {
        return text.to_string();
    }
    
    let ellipsis_width = unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(ellipsis);
    let target_width = max_width.saturating_sub(ellipsis_width);
    
    let mut result = String::new();
    let mut current_width = 0;
    
    for grapheme in text.graphemes(true) {
        let grapheme_width = unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(grapheme);
        
        if current_width + grapheme_width > target_width {
            break;
        }
        
        result.push_str(grapheme);
        current_width += grapheme_width;
    }
    
    result.push_str(ellipsis);
    result
}
```

## Test Cases Required

### Wrapping Tests
- [ ] ASCII text wraps at word boundaries
- [ ] CJK text wraps correctly (each char = 2 cells)
- [ ] Emoji never split mid-sequence
- [ ] ZWJ family emoji (👨‍👩‍👧‍👦) wrap as unit
- [ ] Combining marks stay with base (é)
- [ ] Regional indicators (🇺🇸) wrap as pair
- [ ] Long words wrap mid-word correctly
- [ ] Empty lines preserved

### Truncation Tests
- [ ] Short text unchanged
- [ ] Long ASCII truncated with ellipsis
- [ ] CJK truncated at grapheme boundary
- [ ] Emoji truncated correctly
- [ ] ZWJ sequence truncated as unit
- [ ] Ellipsis fits within width
- [ ] Edge case: width < ellipsis width

### Comprehensive Corpus (from bd-16k)
- [ ] All ASCII printable characters
- [ ] Common CJK (日本語, 中文, 한국어)
- [ ] Common emoji (🎉, ❤️, 🔥, 😀)
- [ ] ZWJ sequences (👨‍💻, 👩‍🔬, 🏳️‍🌈)
- [ ] Skin tone modifiers (👋🏻, 👋🏿)
- [ ] Combining diacritics (é, ñ, ü)
- [ ] Variation selectors
- [ ] Zero-width characters (ZWSP, ZWNJ)

## Dependencies
- Uses unicode-segmentation crate for graphemes()
- Uses unicode-width crate for width measurement
- Integrates with LRU width cache (bd-1oz)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] wrap_text() is grapheme-correct
- [ ] truncate_text() is grapheme-correct
- [ ] All test cases pass
- [ ] Integrated into Text type (bd-2uk)
- [ ] Performance: < 10µs for typical text (cached)

## Location

 {"id":"bd-em01","title":"Performance Tracing with Span Support","description":"# Performance Tracing with Span Support

## Background & Motivation

Performance debugging requires understanding where time goes:
- Which widgets are slow?
- Is it diff computation or presentation?
- Where are the allocation hotspots?

**The solution:**
- Deep integration with `tracing` crate
- Spans for all render phases
- Flamegraph export capability
- Zero overhead when disabled

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Enhances bd-3e8 (structured logging with tracing)
- Complements bd-19x (performance benchmarks)

## Technical Design

```rust
impl Presenter {
    #[tracing::instrument(skip(self, frame), level = "info")]
    pub fn present(&mut self, frame: &Frame) -> io::Result<()> {
        let _diff_span = tracing::info_span!("diff").entered();
        let diff = self.compute_diff(frame);
        drop(_diff_span);
        
        let _emit_span = tracing::info_span!("emit").entered();
        self.emit_diff(&diff)?;
        drop(_emit_span);
        
        Ok(())
    }
}

// Widget render spans
impl Widget for Panel {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let _span = tracing::debug_span!("Panel::render", ?area).entered();
        // ...
    }
}
```

## Span Hierarchy

```
frame
├── layout
│   ├── constraint_solve
│   └── area_assign
├── render
│   ├── Panel::render
│   │   ├── border
│   │   └── content
│   └── Text::render
├── diff
│   └── cell_compare
└── present
    └── ansi_emit
```

## Logging Requirements

All spans automatically logged by tracing subscriber. Key metrics:
- Span duration (automatic)
- Nested span hierarchy (automatic)
- Custom fields (area, cell_count, etc.)

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Generate flamegraph
FTUI_TRACING=flamegraph ./ftui-harness --duration 5s
inferno-flamegraph < ftui.folded > flamegraph.svg

# Assert span presence
./scripts/verify-spans.sh --required "frame,layout,render,diff,present"

# Performance overhead test
./ftui-harness --benchmark --with-tracing --assert-overhead-percent 1
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spans for all render phases (layout, render, diff, present)
- [ ] Widget-level spans with area info
- [ ] Flamegraph compatible output (folded stacks)
- [ ] Configurable granularity (frame/phase/widget)
- [ ] Zero overhead when tracing disabled
- [ ] Overhead < 1% when enabled
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"## Zero Overhead Implementation

To achieve zero overhead when tracing is disabled, use compile-time feature gating:

```rust
#[cfg(feature = "tracing")]
macro_rules! render_span {
    (xpr, *) => {
        tracing::debug_span!(, *).entered()
    };
}

#[cfg(not(feature = "tracing"))]
macro_rules! render_span {
    (xpr, *) => {
        // No-op when tracing disabled
        ()
    };
}

// Usage
impl Widget for Panel {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let _span = render_span!("Panel::render", ?area);
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Feature Configuration

```toml
# Cargo.toml
[features]
default = []
tracing = ["dep:tracing"]
tracing-flamegraph = ["tracing", "dep:tracing-flame"]
```

### Overhead Budget
- When disabled: 0% (compile-time elimination)
- Frame spans only: <0.5% overhead
- Phase spans: <1% overhead
- Widget spans: <2% overhead (configurable)

This should be added to the implementation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:12:18.840871938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:06:57.176153026Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:06:57.176133048Z","close_reason":"Implemented performance tracing spans in Presenter and BufferDiff.
